title: Domain Model
description: Schema for a domain model: a ubiquitous-language glossary, named enums, entities (with relationships, attributes, actions, and invariants), and scenarios that stress-test the model.

kind.description: Identifies the file as a domain model. Always "DomainModel".

version.description: Schema revision this file targets. This v1 schema describes only v1; later versions are published at their own URL (.../v2.json, ...). modelith is the source of truth for which versions a given build supports.

title.description: Optional human-readable title for the model, used as the heading when rendered.

description.description: Optional one-paragraph summary of what this model covers.

glossary.description: Ubiquitous-language terms that are NOT entities — roles, states of being, domain nouns — mapped to their definitions. Any term used as a relationship role or scenario actor should be defined here so the vocabulary is explicit and checkable. Keys are the term in PascalCase (e.g. Owner, Member).

enums.description: First-class enumerated types, keyed by PascalCase name (e.g. ProjectStatus). An attribute selects one by naming it in its "type".

entities.description: The named concepts in the domain, keyed by their canonical PascalCase name.

scenarios.description: Short narratives that exercise entities and actions to stress-test the model.

invariants.description: Model-level rules that span several entities and have no single owner. Same shape as entity invariants; ids share one namespace with entity invariants and must be unique across the whole model.

values.description: The allowed values, each optionally with a definition so its meaning is part of the ubiquitous language.

definition.description: What this value means — so a state like "active" has a single agreed meaning.
Two to four sentences: what it is, what it is not, what distinguishes it.

relationships.description: How this entity relates to others. A relationship is declared under one entity and names a target; it may be declared from either end (the linter checks that the two views agree). When one entity is intuitively the parent — it owns or contains the other, or is the "one" side of a one-to-many — prefer declaring the relationship there.

attributes.description: Key properties of the entity that matter for domain reasoning — not every database column. Mark computed values with derived: true.

actions.description: Mutations the system exposes for this entity. Each is a bare name (e.g. "create") or a structured object linking the action to the actor that performs it and the invariants it must preserve.

invariants.description: Rules that must always hold. Each has a stable id (so scenarios and actions can reference it) and a short, declarative, testable statement.

derived.description: True if this entity is wholly computed from other state rather than persisted — never stored, recomputed on demand. Distinct from an owned relationship: composing a derived entity is usually a modeling error (the linter warns).

derivation.description: How a derived entity is computed. Optional even when derived is true, since the definition often already explains it.

else.$comment: derivation is only allowed when derived: true

entity.description: Name of the related entity. Must reference a defined entity.

role.description: What role the related entity plays (freeform; backtick entity names). A role name like `Owner` should be defined in the glossary.

ownership.description: Whether the related entity is part of this one. "owned" = it cannot exist independently of this entity (composition: it is created within, and deleted with, this entity). "referenced" = an independent entity this one merely points at. Omitting ownership means "referenced".

note.description: Additional freeform note about the relationship.

type.description: Conceptual type: a primitive (e.g. "string", "integer", "boolean", "timestamp") or the PascalCase name of an enum defined under top-level "enums".

derived.description: True if this attribute is computed from other state rather than stored. Forces a "derivation" explaining how. Keeps newcomers from modeling derived values as persisted columns.

derivation.description: How a derived attribute is computed (required when derived is true).

else.$comment: derivation is only allowed when derived: true

action.title: Action
action.description: A mutation the system exposes: either a bare name string or a structured object with actor, preserves, and description.

actionObject.title: Action (structured)
actionObject.description: Structured action linking the mutation to its actor and the invariants it must preserve.

actor.description: Who performs the action — a defined entity or a glossary term/role (e.g. Owner).

preserves.description: Ids of invariants this action must preserve.

id.description: Stable identifier (lowercase kebab-case), referenced by scenarios (invariants_touched) and actions (preserves). Renaming the statement does not break references.

statement.description: The rule. Short, declarative, testable. Backtick entity names.

description.description: Short prose summary of what this scenario tests or demonstrates.

actors.description: The actors involved, named either as a defined entity (e.g. "User") or as a glossary role (e.g. "Owner"). Unlike freeform text, these are not backticked.

steps.description: Ordered steps. Backtick entity names in freeform text.

invariants_touched.description: Ids of invariants this scenario exercises. Each must reference an invariant declared on some entity.
